_meta.description: Parking scenario overlay for Meridian Estate Office (sid=103). Provides slot inventory across both basements, registered vehicles by tenant suite, violation type catalog, and DETERMINISTIC repeat-offender plates. Office-estate parking differs structurally from residential parking: slots are allocated per TENANT COMPANY (not per individual), turnover is much higher (employees, visitors, courier drivers, cab drop-offs), and 'wrong-tenant slot' is the dominant violation type.
design_notes: - Slot allocations are per-suite (tenant company), not per-individual. A suite may allocate its slots to any of its employees; the platform does NOT track employee-vehicle mappings within a tenant.
- Visitor slot duration is SHORTER (90 min) — office visitors are typically meeting attendees, not residential guests.
- 'wrong_tenant_slot' is the most common violation: an employee of Tenant-A parks in a slot allocated to Tenant-B.
- Cab drop-off / pick-up zones have their own etiquette (60 sec stop max, no parking).
- No overnight residential parking — building closes at 23:00 to public access; any vehicle remaining after 23:00 is 'after_hours_unauthorized'.

All vehicle plates use the Indian format STATE-DISTRICT-LETTERS-NUMBERS. Plates for tenant-company-registered vehicles use realistic letter sequences; repeat-offender plates use ZZ/XX/ZX letters for at-a-glance identification.
All owner phones use the reserved test-phone range +9199000XXXXX per Part 0-B Layer 2.

basement_A_north_tower.allocation_rule: tenant suites in North Tower get 1-3 slots each based on suite size + lease terms
basement_A_north_tower._note: Largest tenants (e.g. anchor floors) get 3-4 slots; small suites get 1; some suites get zero.

visitor.rules: max 90 min; expired_visitor violation after 90 min; visitor must be registered at reception

ev_charging.rules: EV vehicles only with active charging session

fire_lane.rules: any vehicle stationary here = fire_lane violation, immediate clamping eligibility

handicap.rules: non-permit holder parked here = hp_lane_misuse violation

executive_reserved.rules: reserved for property-co directors + named tenant CEOs by agreement
executive_reserved._note: Configurable per onboarding — this is the kind of slot type that doesn't exist in residential parking.

cab_dropoff.slots: DROP-001 to DROP-004 (front of each tower)
cab_dropoff.rules: 60-second stop max; no parking; loitering vehicle = cab_zone_loiter violation

violation_types.description: Vehicle of one tenant company parked in another tenant's allocated slot
Vehicle blocks basement exit ramp or tower egress
Non-registered vehicle remaining in basement after 23:00 closure
Vehicle in marked fire lane — immediate clamping authorized
ICE vehicle in EV-charging slot, or EV not actively charging
Vehicle parked / loitering in cab drop-off zone beyond 60-second stop

registered_vehicles_to_seed._note: Office parking has much higher vehicle count than residential — many tenants have multiple registered employee vehicles. The seeder will produce vehicles linked to TENANT COMPANY names (stored in vehicles.owner_name as the company), with the suite as unit_number. No individual employee mapping inside this platform.
registered_vehicles_to_seed.distribution: uneven by tenant — anchor tenants (3 suites combined) register ~10 vehicles each; small single-suite tenants register 1-3

deterministic_repeat_offender_plates._note: Four office-specific repeat-offender plates. Note one is a cab-zone-loiter offender (Ola/Uber driver) and one is a wrong-tenant-slot offender — the two most common office parking issues that don't exist in residential.

plates.expected_system_behavior: should appear in 'repeat offenders' admin view; demonstrates office-specific wrong_tenant_slot pattern
demonstrates office after-hours pattern + clamping authorization audit trail; clamped=1 in vehicles table

violations_seeded._note: Driver moved to visitor slot to wait — also expired

plates.expected_system_behavior: demonstrates non-tenant repeat offender; 4 violations in 30 days well over 5th-major-incident threshold
demonstrates visitor-class repeat offender escalating to exec-slot misuse

live_test_plates._note: Reserved for the tester to use during live testing. Cron WILL fire for these.

plates_reserved_for_live_testing.purpose: Repeat-offender escalation test — file 3 wrong_tenant_slot complaints in quick succession
